name: meditation-cell
summary: A single-occupant cell for meditation or contemplative retreat. Almost bare — a mat, a candle, perhaps a single image. The room's austerity is the practice.

primary_palette.note: a single kneeler facing a bare wall with one candle
a meditation mat on the floor, a candle set just off-center
a single holy image to meditate upon, a kneeler before it
a small devotional idol on a shelf or ledge, a candle and offering below

story_prompts: currently occupied — a hooded figure motionless on the kneeler, unaware of intrusion
long-abandoned — dust on the mat, the candle a wax stub
the occupant in crisis — prayer book flung aside, candle overturned but not lit the floor
an ascetic's cell — a single bowl, a single cloak, nothing else
a cell used by someone hiding — a bedroll wedged against a wall, the shrine kept up as cover

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: very dim (0.1-0.2) — a single candle is the whole lighting plan

anti_patterns: never more than two or three props — austerity is the design
no multi-cell props — everything fits in a one-meter footprint
no seating beyond a stool or kneeler — this is not a room for visitors
